summary.intent: Address two P1 Codex review findings on PR #681 for the TimeField component: controlled-empty semantics and IME composition-end input handling.
summary.outcome: Both Codex findings were resolved: `Props::value` was updated to `Option<Option<Time>>` for controlled-empty semantics matching DateField, and composition start/end hooks were added to commit finalized composed text. All checks passed (unit tests, spec conformance, spec validation, fmt, clippy, proptest, focused branch coverage ≥91.32%), and the fix was committed as `dacca917`'s follow-up and pushed.

repo: TimeField's Props::value uses Option<Option<Time>> internally (matching DateField) to distinguish 'no value prop' (uncontrolled) from 'explicitly cleared value' (controlled-empty).
DateField is the canonical reference implementation for controlled/uncontrolled semantics and IME composition handling in date-time components.

The project uses a Codex review bot that signals review state via GitHub reactions: 'eyes' = reviewing, thumbs-up = approved, dropped eyes without thumbs-up = new findings posted as PR review threads.
Focused branch coverage threshold for TimeField is effectively ~90%; dropping below triggers a CI failure concern.
The project has a spec conformance test suite (`cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance time_field`) and a spec validation tool (`cargo xtask spec validate`) that must both pass.
Proptest state machine tests exist for time field event sequences (`proptest_time_field_event_sequences_preserve_invariants`) and must be run with `--ignored` flag.

code.finding: Props struct updated: value field changed to Option<Option<Time>> to support controlled-empty semantics; builder API kept ergonomic.
Composition start/end hooks added to the connect API; composition-end handler commits finalized numeric/day-period text from IME input.
Tests added for controlled-empty (value stays controlled when set to None) and IME composition-end (composed text is committed as segment input).
Spec updated to document controlled-empty semantics and composition-end behavior.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; reaction tracking (eyes → thumbs-up or dropped eyes + new threads) is the signal for review completion.
The validation stack order before pushing: fmt → unit tests → spec conformance → spec validate → clippy → proptest → llvm-cov branch coverage.
llvm-cov focused branch reports require post-processing with awk/rg to identify uncovered branches in specific files.
Mutation tests are explicitly excluded from Codex review loop passes to save CI time.
After pushing review fixes, Codex review threads must be replied to and resolved manually.

friction: Codex's `eyes` reaction dropped without a thumbs-up, requiring manual inspection of unresolved review threads to determine if findings existed — the bot did not add a final approval reaction.
Focused branch coverage dipped to 89.58% after adding composition paths, requiring additional targeted test coverage before pushing.
Multiple polling iterations (sleep 90s/120s loops) were needed to wait for both Codex review completion and CI run completion, resulting in significant wall-clock wait time.
Coverage report required complex awk/rg post-processing pipeline to isolate uncovered branches in a specific file.
open_items: Mutation tests were explicitly skipped for this Codex review pass and remain unrun for the new composition and controlled-empty code paths.
The Codex review threads need to be manually replied to and resolved after the push (transcript ends before this is confirmed done).
CI run 26418519753 was still in progress at the end of the transcript — final CI green status for the pushed fix commit was not confirmed.
